name: Australian Open
stakes: This week at the Australian Open was a crucial opportunity for India's shuttlers to gain momentum and valuable ranking points on the BWF Tour.
summary:
India's campaign at the Australian Open Super 500 has drawn to a close, with the tournament wrapping up today. While many of the nation's top shuttlers saw their runs end earlier than hoped, the spotlight shone brightly on **PV Sindhu**, who advanced to the semifinals in the Women's Singles. Her deep run underscores her continued fight at the elite level, even as she navigates a challenging season.

Men's Doubles top seeds **Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty**, fresh off their Singapore Open triumph, faced an early exit—a surprising turn for the in-form pair. Similarly, Men's Singles hopefuls Lakshya Sen, HS Prannoy, and rising talent Ayush Shetty, alongside Women's Doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, also concluded their campaigns without reaching the final stages. The event offered valuable match practice and ranking points, but for most, it was a test of resilience rather than a podium finish.
